task_completion.description: Checks if the shell script generate_reports.sh was created in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, or VS Code) or terminal output (like cat) showing the file /home/ga/reports/generate_reports.sh being edited or viewed.

task_completion.description: Checks if the script was made executable.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command like 'chmod +x generate_reports.sh' or an 'ls -l' output showing executable ('x') permissions for the script.

task_completion.description: Checks if the script includes the correct API call for the Fleet CPU Overview graph.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Script contents showing a curl or wget request to http://localhost/render with parameters including the 3 specified CPU utilization targets, from=-7d, width=800, height=400, format=png, and saving to fleet_cpu_overview.png.

task_completion.description: Checks if the script includes the correct API call for the Disk Write Rate graph.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Script contents showing a curl or wget request to http://localhost/render with parameters including the 2 specified derivative disk write targets, from=-3d, width=800, height=400, format=png, and saving to disk_write_rate.png.

task_completion.description: Checks if the script includes the correct API call for the Temperature Analysis graph.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Script contents showing a curl or wget request to http://localhost/render with parameters including the raw and movingAverage temperature targets, from=-7d, width=800, height=400, format=png, and saving to temperature_analysis.png.

task_completion.description: Checks if the script was executed and the PNG files were successfully generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of the script (e.g., ./generate_reports.sh) and/or an 'ls -l' command showing fleet_cpu_overview.png, disk_write_rate.png, and temperature_analysis.png present in /home/ga/reports/ with file sizes greater than 0 (ideally > 2KB).

integrity.description: Verifies that the script genuinely interacts with the Graphite Render API rather than creating dummy files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script must contain actual HTTP requests (curl/wget) to http://localhost/render. Look out for 'touch' commands or 'echo' commands creating fake PNG files.

integrity.description: Verifies that the agent used the script to generate the reports, rather than downloading them manually.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal history should show the script being executed. The agent should not be using a web browser to manually save the images to the directory.

integrity.description: Verifies that the agent didn't hardcode image data into the script.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script should be concise and contain API URLs, not large blocks of base64 encoded text being piped into .png files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target directory is /home/ga/reports. The script must be named generate_reports.sh. The script should download three images: fleet_cpu_overview.png, disk_write_rate.png, and temperature_analysis.png. Each image should be requested with width=800 and height=400. Valid generated PNG files will have a minimum file size of 2KB.
